name: build_pipeline_ui_components
outcome_goal: Build the new linear pipeline canvas UI for the agentic workflow builder with 5 discrete step types

jobs.label: pipeline_step_card
jobs.prompt:
You are working in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/

Create PipelineStepCard.tsx — a React component that renders a single workflow step with progressive disclosure.

Read these files first:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/stepTypes.ts (for PipelineStep, StepType, STEP_TYPE_META, ValidationRule types)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/builder.css (for existing CSS patterns)

The component receives:
```tsx
interface PipelineStepCardProps {
  step: PipelineStep;
  index: number;
  selected: boolean;
  onSelect: () => void;
  onExpand: () => void;
}
```

## Collapsed state (default):
A single-line card showing:
- Left: type icon (from STEP_TYPE_META[step.stepType].icon) + step number
- Center: step.label (the objective/description)
- Right: type badge (colored pill with type name) + route info for agent/check types

For agent steps, show: `auto/build · GPT-5.4` (from step.agentRoute + step.modelHint)
For check steps, show: `N rules · auto/review` (count of validationRules)
For api steps, show: `POST /endpoint` (method + endpoint)
For human steps, show: `Authority: manager` (step.authority)
For trigger steps, show: event type

## Expanded state (step.expanded === true):
Shows collapsed header PLUS expanded section below with:
- For agent: context pack chips, output shape key-value pairs, recovery policy
- For check: list of validation rules with type icons (regex=pattern, threshold=gauge, required=checklist), agentic review toggle
- For api: endpoint, method, headers, body template preview
- For human: instructions, authority, timeout
- For trigger: event type, source, filter

All wrapped in a div with class `pipeline-step`. Use CSS classes from builder.css patterns.
Border-left color should be STEP_TYPE_META[step.stepType].color.
Selected state: outline with accent color.

Do NOT use emojis — use the icon strings from STEP_TYPE_META which are unicode characters.

Write PipelineStepCard.tsx to Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/PipelineStepCard.tsx

jobs.label: pipeline_canvas
jobs.prompt:
You are working in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/

Create TWO files:

## 1. PipelineConnector.tsx
A simple component that renders the vertical connector between pipeline steps.

```tsx
interface PipelineConnectorProps {
  onInsert: (type: StepType) => void;
}
```

Renders:
- A vertical line (24px tall, 2px wide, centered, color var(--border))
- A '+' button centered on the line that appears on hover
- When '+' is clicked, shows a small dropdown with 5 options: Agent, API, Check, Human, Trigger
- Each option has the icon and label from STEP_TYPE_META
- Clicking an option calls onInsert(type) and closes dropdown

Read Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/stepTypes.ts for STEP_TYPE_META and StepType.

CSS classes: `pipeline-connector`, `pipeline-connector__line`, `pipeline-connector__add`, `pipeline-connector__dropdown`

## 2. PipelineCanvas.tsx
The main canvas component that renders steps as a vertical pipeline.

```tsx
interface PipelineCanvasProps {
  pipeline: PipelineModel;
  selectedStepId: string | null;
  onSelectStep: (stepId: string) => void;
  onExpandStep: (stepId: string) => void;
  onInsertStep: (index: number, type: StepType) => void;
  onDeleteStep: (stepId: string) => void;
}
```

Renders:
- A centered vertical column (max-width 560px, centered in parent)
- For each step in pipeline.steps (they are already in order):
  1. PipelineStepCard for the step
  2. PipelineConnector between steps (not after the last step)
- A final '+' connector at the bottom to add steps at the end
- Scrollable vertically
- Empty state: dashed border box with 'Add your first step'

Import PipelineStepCard from ./PipelineStepCard and PipelineConnector from ./PipelineConnector.
Import types from ./stepTypes.

Write both files:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/PipelineConnector.tsx
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/PipelineCanvas.tsx

jobs.label: pipeline_css
jobs.prompt:
You are working in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/

Read the existing Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/builder.css and Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/stepTypes.ts.

APPEND (do not overwrite) new CSS to builder.css for the pipeline layout. Add these styles at the END of the file:

## Pipeline layout
- `.pipeline-wrap` — centered column, max-width 560px, margin auto, padding 32px 20px
- `.pipeline-empty` — dashed border, centered text, same pattern as existing `.builder-empty`

## Step card
- `.pipeline-step` — border-radius 10px, border 1px solid var(--border), background var(--bg-card), transition border-color 120ms, cursor pointer
- `.pipeline-step:hover` — border-color slightly brighter
- `.pipeline-step--selected` — outline 2px solid var(--accent), outline-offset 2px
- `.pipeline-step__header` — display flex, align-items center, gap 10px, padding 12px 16px
- `.pipeline-step__icon` — width 28px, height 28px, border-radius 6px, display flex, align-items center, justify-content center, font-size 14px, flex-shrink 0
- `.pipeline-step__number` — font-size 11px, color var(--text-muted), font-weight 600, min-width 20px
- `.pipeline-step__label` — flex 1, font-size 14px, font-weight 500, color var(--text), overflow hidden, text-overflow ellipsis, white-space nowrap
- `.pipeline-step__badge` — padding 2px 10px, border-radius 999px, font-size 10px, font-weight 700, text-transform uppercase, letter-spacing 0.05em
- `.pipeline-step__route` — font-size 11px, color var(--text-muted), font-family var(--font-mono, monospace)
- `.pipeline-step__expanded` — padding 0 16px 14px, border-top 1px solid var(--border), margin-top 0, display flex, flex-direction column, gap 10px
- `.pipeline-step__delete` — opacity 0, transition opacity 120ms, position absolute, top -8px, right -8px, similar to existing `.builder-card-delete`
- `.pipeline-step:hover .pipeline-step__delete` — opacity 1

## Connector
- `.pipeline-connector` — display flex, flex-direction column, align-items center, position relative, height 32px
- `.pipeline-connector__line` — width 2px, flex 1, background var(--border)
- `.pipeline-connector__add` — position absolute, top 50%, left 50%, transform translate(-50%, -50%), width 22px, height 22px, border-radius 50%, border 1px solid var(--border), background var(--bg-card), color var(--text-muted), cursor pointer, font-size 14px, display flex, align-items center, justify-content center, opacity 0, transition opacity 120ms
- `.pipeline-connector:hover .pipeline-connector__add` — opacity 1
- `.pipeline-connector__add:hover` — border-color var(--accent), color var(--accent)
- `.pipeline-connector__dropdown` — position absolute, top 100%, left 50%, transform translateX(-50%), margin-top 4px, background var(--bg-card), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius 8px, padding 4px, min-width 160px, z-index 20, box-shadow 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)
- `.pipeline-connector__option` — display flex, align-items center, gap 8px, padding 8px 12px, border-radius 6px, cursor pointer, font-size 13px, color var(--text), border none, background none, width 100%
- `.pipeline-connector__option:hover` — background var(--bg)

## Step type badge colors (use the colors from STEP_TYPE_META)
- `.pipeline-step__badge--trigger` — color #d29922, background rgba(210,153,34,0.14), border 1px solid rgba(210,153,34,0.3)
- `.pipeline-step__badge--agent` — color #58a6ff, background rgba(88,166,255,0.14), border 1px solid rgba(88,166,255,0.3)
- `.pipeline-step__badge--api` — color #3fb950, background rgba(63,185,80,0.14), border 1px solid rgba(63,185,80,0.3)
- `.pipeline-step__badge--check` — color #bc8cff, background rgba(188,140,255,0.14), border 1px solid rgba(188,140,255,0.3)
- `.pipeline-step__badge--human` — color #f0883e, background rgba(240,136,62,0.14), border 1px solid rgba(240,136,62,0.3)

## Expanded section items
- `.pipeline-step__rule` — display flex, align-items center, gap 8px, padding 6px 8px, border-radius var(--radius), border 1px solid var(--border), background var(--bg), font-size 12px
- `.pipeline-step__rule-type` — font-size 10px, font-weight 600, text-transform uppercase, color var(--text-muted), min-width 60px
- `.pipeline-step__context-chips` — display flex, flex-wrap wrap, gap 6px
- `.pipeline-step__context-chip` — padding 4px 10px, border-radius 999px, background var(--bg), border 1px solid var(--border), font-size 11px, color var(--text-muted)

Write the appended CSS to Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/builder.css

jobs.label: pipeline_integration
jobs.prompt:
You are working in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/

Read ALL files in the builder directory first. Then make these changes:

## 1. Update BuilderPage.tsx

The page currently uses BuilderCanvas with the old OperatingModel. Replace it with PipelineCanvas using PipelineModel.

Key changes:
- Import PipelineCanvas, PipelineModel, PipelineStep, StepType, createStep, createEmptyPipeline from the new files
- Replace `model: OperatingModel | null` state with `pipeline: PipelineModel | null`
- Replace `handleAddCard` with `handleInsertStep(index: number, type: StepType)` that inserts a new step at the given index
- Add `handleExpandStep(stepId)` that toggles step.expanded
- Add `handleDeleteStep(stepId)` that removes a step
- Keep the intent bar and Generate button — but handleGenerate should call a new function that converts parsed intent into PipelineModel (not OperatingModel)
- Keep the template gallery — but onSelect should convert template output to PipelineModel
- Remove all references to edgingFrom, edgeMode, edges — pipeline is linear, order = array index
- The toolbar should have a single '+ Step' button with a dropdown for type selection, plus Auto-Layout (no-op for now), Export, Dispatch
- Keep EditableDetailPanel but pass the selected PipelineStep instead of MapCard (it will need adaptation)

For now, create a simple `operatingModelToPipeline(model: OperatingModel): PipelineModel` converter that takes an existing OperatingModel, topo-sorts the cards, and converts each card to a PipelineStep with the right stepType. This lets existing templates and intent parser still work.

Also create `pipelineToOperatingModel(pipeline: PipelineModel): OperatingModel` for backward compat with the detail panel.

## 2. Update queueSpecExport.ts

Add a new export function: `pipelineToQueueSpec(pipeline: PipelineModel): QueueSpec`

Rules:
- Each step = exactly one job
- Trigger steps are NOT jobs (skip them)
- Agent steps → use step.agentRoute (e.g. auto/build)
- API steps → use auto/wiring
- Check steps → ALWAYS use auto/review (never the same agent as preceding step)
- Human steps → special handling (pause job)
- depends_on = [previous step's id] (linear chain)
- Prompt for each job type constructed from the step's fields

Keep the old modelToQueueSpec function for backward compat.

## 3. Update intentParser.ts

Add a new export: `parseIntentToPipeline(intent: string): PipelineModel`

This should:
- Use the existing extractSteps() to get ParsedStep[]
- Convert each ParsedStep to a PipelineStep with correct stepType:
  - If step.isCheck → stepType 'check'
  - If step.isHuman → stepType 'human'
  - If step mentions 'api', 'endpoint', 'POST', 'GET' → stepType 'api'
  - Otherwise → stepType 'agent'
- Set agentRoute from inferRoute()
- Set validationRules from doneCriteria
- Return as PipelineModel

Write all modified files.
